memory.summary: I have an afternoon but the project is barely started; I need to focus on that before leisure now. Skipping it feels risky.
I realized procrastination is compounding my Thursday stress, but the project is still at the start. I need to stop slipping away and work now.

desires: Get the term project to 100% immediately so I can claim total victory and stop caring about anything else.
Stop feeling guilty for sleeping yesterday and demand that my brain gets actual rest before it breaks.

goals.description: Finish the Term project to exactly 100% immediately. No more deliberation, no more 'maybe later.' I need this done now to close the loop.
goals.rationale: The sleep debt is cleared (0.0), so my physical stamina is restored. Now that the biological constraint is gone, the only thing left is the academic one. My value system prioritizes this outcome above all else; feeling guilty about yesterday's rest doesn't matter if it means I can't deliver a perfect result today. I'm not negotiating with myself anymore—I'm executing.

goals.description: Execute the quick_chore and claim the win instantly, then discard the task entirely without further thought or reflection.
goals.rationale: This is low yield compared to the Term project. The progress delta is negligible, and doing it now just adds noise to my schedule before the big finish. I'll do it if the deadline looms in hours, but right now, my focus is strictly on the high-leverage victory. If I feel an urge to do it, I'll acknowledge it and move on.

agent.last_reasoning: This advances g1 (Term project) directly to 100% completion, which is my highest priority. The side effect of energy loss is negligible compared to the academic victory; I don't negotiate with myself about fatigue when the deadline is looming.
